note:
Spotify现在可以在Epic Games Store上购买，这表明开发人员Epic的雄心壮志是将迄今为止严格的游戏商店转变为更通用的应用程序分发平台。 Spotify应用程序可以在Epic Games Store上免费下载，就像在App Store，Google Play上一样，也可以直接下载到PC或Mac上。
 Spotify在Epic Games Store上的可用性即将到来，因为Epic与苹果公司和Google之间就各自公司各自的应用程序商店的商业模式进行了长时间的法律斗争。 Epic已就移除Fortnite的移动版提起诉讼，起诉两家公司，指控其违反了反托拉斯法，并争辩说开发商可以而且应该能够处理自己的付款，并规避将应用内总收入的30％提供给商店所有者。
 Epic Games Store仍然是一个相对较新的数字发行平台，于2018年12月作为Valve的Steam竞争对手推出。但是从一跃而起，Epic为开发人员提供了更为慷慨的付款，其收入仅减少了开发人员收入的12％，而不是在很多情况下由Valve，苹果和Google收取的30％。
  截至1月份，Epic Games Store拥有超过1亿用户，并且大概是由于Epic大量的销售和赠品而使这一数目有所增加。 （五月份的GTA V赠品是如此受欢迎，以至于导致数小时的商店停运。）
 随着Epic Game Store的用户群不断增长，开发人员友好的费用结构以及现在托管着世界上最受欢迎的应用程序之一的事实，Epic似乎在证明非游戏开发人员应将其发行平台视为替代其他应用商店的可行选择。
note_en:
Spotify is  now available on the Epic Games Store, signaling developer Epic’s ambitions to turn what has so far been strictly a games store into a more general app distribution platform. The Spotify app is free to download on the Epic Games Store, just like it is on the App Store, Google Play, or as a direct download for your PC or Mac.
 Spotify’s availability on the Epic Games Store arrives as Epic is in prolonged legal battles with Apple and Google over the business models of each company’s respective app store. Epic has sued  both  companies over the removal of the mobile version of  Fortnite,  alleging antitrust violations and arguing that developers can and should be able to process their own payments and circumvent giving 30 percent of all in-app revenue to the store owner.
 The Epic Games Store is still a relatively new digital distribution platform, having launched in  December 2018 as a rival to Valve’s Steam. But from the jump, Epic offered more generous payouts to developers, taking  only 12 percent of a developer’s revenues for its cut instead of the 30 percent taken in many cases by Valve, as well as Apple and Google.
  As of January, the Epic Games Store  has more than 100 million users, and presumably, that number has grown due in part to  Epic’s plentiful sales and giveaways. (A  GTA V giveaway in May was so popular that it resulted in a  multi-hour store outage.)
 With the Epic Game Store’s growing user base, developer-friendly fee structure, and the fact that it now hosts one of the most popular apps in the world, Epic seems to be making the case that non-gaming developers should consider its distribution platform as a viable alternative to other app stores.
title: Spotify现在可在Epic Games Store上购买，这表明Epic的雄心壮志，即将其以游戏为主的商店转变为通用应用商店
title_en: Spotify is now available on the Epic Games Store, signaling Epic's ambitions to make its game-focused store into a general app store

note:
游戏引擎制造商Unity已与Snap合作，以扩展其广告和技术平台的覆盖范围。
 该交易将把Unity的广告供应范围扩展到Snapchat广告客户，并通过Snap Kit工具将Snap技术带给游戏开发人员。这是另一个迹象，表明游戏在当今比以往任何时候都更为主流，并且是共享的社会消费者体验的一部分。
  该联盟于2020年下半年开始运作，是巨人的伙伴关系。超过50％的手机游戏使用Unity，Snapchat的广告和游戏集成每天达到2.49亿用户。
 朱莉·舒马克（Julie Shumaker）是Unity收入和运营解决方案副总裁，她在给GamesBeat的电子邮件中说，Snap消息传递服务的大型广告社区将非常适合Unity的高度参与和有价值的玩家观众。联盟应推动社交共享和游戏发现。
 Shumaker说：“我们俩都专注于最适合用户的东西，在这种合作关系中，用户既是开发人员又是玩家。” “开发人员将受益于SAN竞标Unity时增加的广告需求，并且Snap Kit集成为他们带来了玩家之间社交游戏发现的更多好处。”
 从今天开始，Unity Ads现在已包含在Snap Audience Network（SAN）中。 Snapchat的SAN广告客户活动现在将包括Unity广泛的移动游戏标题网络中的视频广告资源，从而帮助广告客户扩展到Snapchat之外。 Unity Ads报告的全球每月广告展示量超过229亿，通过使用其引擎的游戏开发人员，每月的活跃用户超过20亿。
 到2020年，由于大流行，移动广告查看器的转换率提高了，安装转换率提高了23％，移动游戏安装量增加了84％。 Snap的Ben Schwerin在发给GamesBeat的电子邮件中表示，Snap于2019年4月推出了游戏，由于游戏已成为人们越来越多地在Snapchat上参与的共享活动，因此下载量已超过1亿。 
Schwerin说：“借助Snap Kit，我们已经集成了数百种应用程序，但我们还没有直接关注游戏市场-与Snap Audience Network（SAN）一样。” “我们借此机会进入了游戏中最大的平台Unity。”
 现在，Unity Asset Store中提供了移动游戏开发人员，他们现在还可以利用Snap Kit的精选功能来增强游戏玩法和游戏发现体验。开发人员可以使用Snap Kit的Login Kit来允许游戏玩家使用其Snapchat帐户作为注册和登录游戏的快速方法。 Snap Kit的Creative Kit扩展了体验，它允许用户共享他们的游戏玩法，使用品牌贴纸装饰静态照片或15秒视频，或者附加使用游戏品牌创建的AR镜头，以便与Snapchat朋友共享。
 Snap的Bitmoji头像将于2021年初与Unity集成，并将通过类似于卡通的头像将游戏的个性化水平提高到新水平。借助Bitmoji for Game的Unity SDK，开发人员将能够使用3D Bitmoji在Unity制作的游戏中创造更身临其境的体验。
note_en:
Game engine maker  Unity has teamed up with  Snap to extend the reach of its ads and technology platform.
 The deal will extend the reach of Unity’s Ads supply to Snapchat advertisers, and it brings Snap technology to game developers through Snap Kit tools. It’s another sign that gaming is more mainstream today than ever and part of a shared social consumer experiences.
  The alliance has been in the works during the second half of 2020, and it’s a partnership of giants. More than 50% of all mobile games use Unity, and Snapchat has advertising and game integrations that reach its audience of 249 million people every day.
 Julie Shumaker is the vice president of revenue and operate solutions at Unity, and she said in an email to GamesBeat that Snap’s big advertising community on its messaging service will be a good fit with Unity’s highly engaged and valuable player audiences. The alliance should drive social sharing and game discovery.
 “We both focus on what’s best for users, and in this partnership, users are both developers and players,” Shumaker said. “Developers will benefit from both increased ads demand with SAN bidding on Unity supply, and the Snap Kit integration provides them added benefit with social game discovery between players.”
 Starting today, Unity Ads is now included in the Snap Audience Network (SAN). Snapchat’s SAN advertiser campaigns will now include video inventory from Unity’s extensive network of mobile gaming titles, helping advertisers extend beyond Snapchat. Unity Ads reports 22.9 billion-plus monthly global ad impressions, and it reaches more than two billion monthly active users through game developers who use its engine.
 In 2020, mobile ad viewers have converted at higher frequencies, with install conversion rates up by 23%, and mobile gamers installing 84% more apps, thanks to the pandemic. Snap launched games in April 2019, and it has reached more than 100 million game downloads as gaming becomes a shared activity that people increasingly engage in on Snapchat, said Snap’s Ben Schwerin in an email to GamesBeat.
 “With Snap Kit, we’ve integrated with hundreds of apps, but we haven’t paid attention to the game market directly yet — same with Snap Audience Network (SAN),” Schwerin said. “We took this opportunity to go to the biggest platform in games — Unity.”
 Available today in the Unity Asset Store, mobile game developers can also now leverage select features of Snap Kit to enhance gameplay and the game discovery experience. Developers can use Snap Kit’s Login Kit to allows gamer to use their Snapchat account as a quick way to sign up and log in to games. And Snap Kit’s Creative Kit extends the experience by allowing users to share their gameplay, decorating still shots or 15-second videos with branded stickers, or attaching an AR lens that has been created with game branding to share with their Snapchat friends.
 Snap’s Bitmoji avatars will be integrated with Unity in early 2021 and will add a new level of personalization to gameplay via cartoon-like avatars. With Bitmoji for Game’s Unity SDK, developers will be able to use 3D Bitmoji to create a more immersive experience in games made with Unity.
title: Unity与Snap合作扩展了广告的覆盖面，并将SnapKit集成到Unity的游戏引擎中，包括2021年的Bitmoji
title_en: Unity partners with Snap to extend the reach of Unity's ads and integrate SnapKit in Unity's game engine, including Bitmoji in 2021

法官已命令苹果出示蒂姆·库克（Tim Cook）和克雷格·费德里希（Craig Federighi）作证，要求苹果对Epic提起诉讼，他们必须在下一次聆讯前出示所需的文件。
  随着公开提交的法院文件告诉我们有关即将进行的审判的更多信息，Apple与Epic的诉讼仍在继续。 Epic希望苹果公司在App Store及其操作方面产生大量文档，但是对于该数据需要多大范围以及由谁来呈现，人们已经进行了一些讨论。
 提交的文件指出，苹果将负担沉重的负担，以收集Epic要求的大部分内容。苹果公司在这里向法院表示，除非绝对必要，否则Epic无需索取更多放大数据。
 法院文件中最重要的部分是将由谁来代表Apple。 Epic已要求让蒂姆·库克（Tim Cook）和克雷格·费德里希（Craig Federighi）参加聆讯。
 苹果公司表示蒂姆·库克（Tim Cook）可以上班，但要求他的发言时间不得超过四个小时。苹果还要求运行App Store并向Craig Federighi报告的Eric Neuenshwander代替。
  法官拒绝了这两个请求，并指出，直到Epic从苹果公司收到所有适当的文件之前，Tim Cook的时间限制才能确定。对Neuenshwander的请求也被拒绝，法官认为，如果Epic认为Federighi在所有iOS上的领导地位更为重要，那么对于Neuenshwander的重要性他们是否错是Epic的。
 下一个提交文件的截止日期是2021年1月6日，讨论专家的声明，听证会在1月8日举行。这只是向前迈出的小步，因为我们正在等待下一次听证会，时间定在2021年7月。 
AppleInsider具有会员合作伙伴关系，并可能通过会员链接购买的产品赚取佣金。 这些伙伴关系不会影响我们的社论内容。
note_en:
A judge has ordered Apple to produce Tim Cook and Craig Federighi to testify for the  Apple versus Epic lawsuit, and they must produce required documents before the next hearing.
  The Apple versus Epic lawsuit continues as publicly filed court documents tell us a bit more about the upcoming trial. Epic wants Apple to produce extensive documentation surrounding the  App Store and its operations, but there has been some deliberation as to how extensive this data needs to be, and who will present it.
 The document filed states that Apple will have a large burden placed on them to gather much of what Epic is asking for. The court sides with Apple here stating that Epic need not ask for more amplifying data unless absolutely necessary.
 The most important part of the court filing is who's going to be made available to represent Apple. Epic has requested that  Tim Cook and  Craig Federighi be made available for the hearing.
 Apple says that Tim Cook will be available, but requests his deposition be limited to four hours. Apple also requested that Eric Neuenshwander, who runs the App Store and reports to Craig Federighi, be present instead.
  The judge denied both requests, stating that Tim Cook's time limitations will not be determined until Epic receive all the proper documents from Apple. The request for Neuenshwander was also denied, with the judge reasoning that if Epic sees Federighi's leadership position over all  iOS as more significant, then it is on Epic if they are wrong about Neuenshwander's significance.
 The next deadline for court filings is January 6, 2021 to discuss expert declarations, with a hearing on January 8. This is just another small step forward as we  await the next hearing set for July 2021.
    AppleInsider has affiliate partnerships and may earn commission on products purchased through affiliate links. These partnerships do not influence our editorial content.
title: 法官命令苹果公司出品蒂姆·库克和克雷格·费德里希，在苹果与史诗般的诉讼中作证。 苹果希望蒂姆·库克的证词限于四个小时
title_en: Judge orders Apple to produce Tim Cook and Craig Federighi to testify in Apple vs. Epic lawsuit; Apple wants Tim Cook's testimony to be limited to four hours

note:
Discord又筹集了1亿美元的资金，因为受到游戏玩家欢迎的即时消息传递应用程序现已达到每月1.4亿活跃用户。 Greenoaks Capital领导了这笔资金，这是在Discord于6月份筹集1亿美元资金之后开始的，当时该公司试图覆盖更广泛的社区。
 Discord还表示，其用户群约为一年前的两倍。到目前为止，该公司迄今已筹集了4.793亿美元，其投资者包括Index Ventures，Greylock，IVP和Spark Capital。
  首席执行官Jason Citron在一份声明中说：“我们在如此众多令人难以置信的多元化社区中成长，我们感到无比自豪和荣幸，这些社区使Discord成为了他们闲逛的地方。” “展望2021年，我们对自己的存货感到兴奋，并计划使用这笔资金来帮助Discord变得更好-无论是为我们的免费服务还是为Nitro订户。”
 Greenoaks Capital的执行合伙人Neil Mehta在一份声明中说，Discord已经成为您与社区聚会的地方，无论是玩视频游戏，交换食谱还是在项目上进行合作。他说，他的公司相信Discord会随着人们发现新的互动方式而发展和壮大。
 自2015年成立以来，Discord的吸引力始终在于其小型的，类似客厅的氛围，在这里，成群的朋友可以一起聊天游戏。随着Discord的发展，它已成为主流，为每个人提供了一种谈论巨大激情的方式，包括体育，桌面游戏，音乐，宠物和新闻。该公司拥有超过250名员工。
note_en:
Discord has raised another $100 million in funding as the instant messaging app popularized by gamers has now hit 140 million monthly active users. Greenoaks Capital led the funding, and it comes after Discord previously raised $100 million in June as it tries to reach a broader set of communities.
 Discord also said its user base is about double what it was a year ago. The company has now raised $479.3 million to date, and its investors include Index Ventures, Greylock, IVP, and Spark Capital.
  “We are humbled and honored by the growth we’ve seen among so many incredible and diverse communities that have made Discord their place to hang out,” said CEO Jason Citron in a statement. “As we look to 2021, we are excited about what we have in store and plan to use this funding to help make Discord even better — both for our free service and our Nitro subscribers.”
 Neil Mehta, managing partner at Greenoaks Capital, said in a statement that Discord has become a place where you gather with your communities, whether to play video games, swap recipes, or collaborate on a project. He said his firm believes Discord will evolve and grow as it finds new ways for people to interact.
 Since its founding in 2015, Discord’s appeal has always been in its small, living room-like atmosphere, where groups of friends can join together to talk games. As Discord has grown, it has gone mainstream, providing everyone a way to chat about big passions — including sports, tabletop games, music, pets, and news. The company has more than 250 employees.
title: Discord筹集了1亿美元，由Greenoaks Capital领投，使其总筹资额达到4.793亿美元，并表示现在拥有1.4亿个MAU
title_en: Discord raises $100M led by Greenoaks Capital, bringing its total raised to $479.3M, and says it now has 140M MAUs

日内瓦（美联社）-瑞士当局表示，送餐服务Uber Eats可以算作邮政服务提供商，因此需要受到监管。
 瑞士邮政监管局PostCom周四宣布，这家美国公司的瑞士分支机构需要在1月底前注册为邮政服务提供商，尽管它可以对这一决定提出异议。
 经过近一年的评估，PostCom发现Uber Eats的业务至少有一部分属于邮政法规。邮包在本质上符合邮政服务的标准，“邮政的内容与包裹的属性无关”，邮政通讯在一份声明中说。
  活跃于瑞士的子公司Uber Portier B.V.必须在1月30日之前在PostCom进行注册，否则将受邮政法规的约束。邮电公司说，这可能会影响工作时间，工资和该行业其他“正常工作条件”。
 Uber Eats在一份声明中说，“它只是刚刚收到PostCom的论点，现在将对其进行分析，以便评估后续步骤。”
 PostCom称，此前，Uber Switzerland质疑其服从瑞士邮政法的义务，称其与交付该产品的餐厅没有合同关系。
 此举是送餐服务业蓬勃发展的一年：Uber上个月表示，其Eats业务在第三季度产生了14.5亿美元的收入，比去年同期增长了125％，这是因为餐厅依赖该服务，而且人们点菜的趋势有所增加而不是在COVID-19大流行期间外出就餐。
note_en:
GENEVA (AP) — Swiss authorities say food delivery service Uber Eats counts as a postal service provider -- and needs to be regulated as such.
 Switzerland’s postal supervisory authority, PostCom, announced Thursday that the U.S.-based company’s Swiss affiliate needs to register as a postal service provider by the end of January, though it can contest the decision.
 Following a nearly year-long assessment, PostCom found that at least part of Uber Eats’ business falls under postal legislation. Food packages in essence meet the criteria for postal service, and “the nature of the contents isn’t relevant when it comes to the properties of a package,” PostCom said in a statement.
  Uber Portier B.V., the subsidiary active in Switzerland, has until Jan. 30 to register with PostCom and by doing so will become subject to postal regulations. That could have an impact on work hours, salaries and other “usual work conditions in the sector,” PostCom said.
 Uber Eats said in a statement that it had “only just received the argumentation from PostCom and will now analyze it, in order to evaluate the next steps.”
 Previously, Uber Switzerland challenged the obligation to subject itself to Swiss postal law, arguing that it was not in contractual relationship with restaurants for which the deliveries were made, PostCom said.
 The move comes in a boom year for the food delivery service: Uber said last month its Eats business generated $1.45 billion in revenue in the third quarter, up 125% from a year ago as restaurants relied on the service and the trend grew of people ordering in instead of dining out during the COVID-19 pandemic.
title: 瑞士表示，Uber Eats必须在1月30日之前注册为邮政服务提供商，这样做将受到邮政法规的约束； 优步可以竞争
title_en: Switzerland says Uber Eats must register as a postal service provider before Jan. 30 and by doing so will become subject to postal regulations; Uber can contest

note:
在线服装零售商Poshmark在过去两个季度的利润超过3000万美元之后，于周四提交了IPO招股说明书。
 Poshmark成立于2011年，是二手服装，鞋子和配饰的互联网市场。与eBay一样，Poshmark也将买家与卖家联系起来，卖家通常会在自己的壁橱中列出商品。该公司通过减少每笔交易来赚钱。
  Poshmark的文件正在登陆投资者在上周同时也是市场业务的DoorDash和Airbnb的IPO上市几圈之后，首日出现了大笔交易，这可能表明公众对这种商业模式的兴趣。折扣在线零售商Wish本周进行了首次公开募股，尽管其股价跌跌撞撞。
 Poshmark的收入在2020年前三个季度中增长了28％，从去年同期的1.505亿美元增长至1.928亿美元。在那一年的亏损3390万美元之后，该公司扭亏为盈，获得了2090万美元的利润。
 在第一季度，由于冠状病毒大流行，总商品数量是衡量在线销售商品总价值的一项关键指标，因此受到负面影响。在今年的前三个月中，它仅增长了9％，但是在第二季度，随着买卖活动的恢复，反弹至42％。该公司还列举了其不断增长的活跃买家群体，该数量在两年前的6月翻了一番。
 与许多在线零售商一样，波什马克说，这得益于冠状病毒产生的大量需求，因为地方政府命令人们呆在室内，而零售店也关闭了。该公司补充说，该市场还为Poshmark的450万卖家提供了额外的收入来源。
 但是，Poshmark表示，由于对消费者的经济影响以及整个经济稳定性的不确定性，Covid-19仍然存在风险。 
＆＃34;对COVID-19大流行的回应，例如延长的在家工作政策，隔离，关闭和旅行限制，可能会继续抑制我们平台上出售的产品的需求，＆＃34; 该公司在招股说明书中说。  该文件是对Poshmark财务状况的首次了解，该公司于9月份秘密提交了上市申请。 它计划在纳斯达克上市，股票代码为“ #POSH”。  Poshmark表示，目前有620万活跃购买者和3170万活跃用户，其中大多数是女性，并且是千禧一代或Z世代。它在竞争对手中列出了Amazon，eBay，Etsy，Facebook，Shopify，TJ Maxx和Walmart。
note_en:
Online clothing reseller Poshmark  filed its IPO prospectus on Thursday, after racking up over $30 million in profit over the past two quarters.
 Poshmark, founded in 2011, is an internet marketplace for second-hand clothing, shoes and accessories. Like  eBay, Poshmark connects buyers with sellers, who often list items from their own closet. The company makes money by taking a cut of each transaction.
  Poshmark's filing is landing in investors' laps after last week's IPOs of  DoorDash and  Airbnb, which are also marketplace businesses, resulted in huge first-day pops, potentially indicating public market appetite for the business model. Discount online retailer  Wish followed with its IPO this week, though its stock price fell out of the gate.
 Revenue at Poshmark increased 28% in the first three quarters of 2020 to $192.8 million from $150.5 million the same period last year. It swung to a profit of $20.9 million over that stretch, after losing $33.9 million a year ago.
 Gross merchandise volume, a key metric measuring total dollar value of merchandise sold online, was negatively impacted in the first quarter because of the coronavirus pandemic. It increased just 9% in the first three months of the year, but rebounded to 42% growth in the second quarter as buyer and seller activity resumed. The company also cited its growing base of active buyers, which doubled in June from two years ago.
 Like many online retailers, Poshmark said it's benefited from a flood of demand generated by the coronavirus, as local governments ordered people to stay indoors and retail stores closed. The marketplace has also served as a source of additional income for Poshmark's 4.5 million sellers, the company added.
 However, Poshmark said that Covid-19 remains a risk because of the economic impact on consumers and uncertainty about the stability of the broader economy.
  "Responses to the COVID-19 pandemic such as prolonged work-from-home policies, quarantines, closures, and travel restrictions could continue to depress demand for the products sold on our platform," the company said in the prospectus.
 The filing provides the first look into Poshmark's financials after the company  confidentially filed to go public in September. It plans to list on the Nasdaq under the symbol "POSH."
 Poshmark said it now counts 6.2 million active buyers and 31.7 million active users, the majority of which are female and are either millennials or Gen Z. It lists  Amazon,  eBay,  Etsy,  Facebook,  Shopify, TJ Maxx and  Walmart among its competitors.
title: Poshmark首次公开募股申请S-1，报告2020年前9个月的收入同比增长28％至1.928亿美元，活跃用户3170万，目前已实现盈利
title_en: Poshmark files S-1 for its IPO, reports revenue growth of 28% YoY to $192.8M in the first nine months of 2020 and 31.7M active users, says it is now profitable

More than three million internet users are believed to have installed 15 Chrome, and 13 Edge extensions that contain malicious code, security firm Avast said today.
     The 28 extensions contained code that could perform several malicious operations. Avast said it found code to:
   But despite the presence of code to power all the above malicious features, Avast researchers said they believe the primary objective of this campaign was to hijack user traffic for monetary gains.
 "For every redirection to a third party domain, the cybercriminals would receive a payment,"  the company said.
 Avast said it discovered the extensions last month and found evidence that some had been active since at least December 2018, when some users first started reporting issues with being redirected to other sites.
 Jan Rubín, Malware Researcher at Avast, said they couldn't identify if the extensions had been created with malicious code from the beginning or if the code was added via an update when each extension passed a level of popularity.
   And many extensions did become very popular, with tens of thousands of installs. Most did so by posing as add-ons meant to help users download multimedia content from various social networks, such as Facebook, Instagram, Vimeo, or Spotify.
 Avast said it reported its findings to both Google and Microsoft and that both companies are still investigating the extensions.
 Google did not return a request for comment seeking additional information on the status of their investigation into Avast's report or if the extensions were going to be removed. Microsoft said it's still investigating the issue.
 A day after Avast published its findings, only three of the 15 Chrome extensions were removed, while all the Edge add-ons were still available for download. A source familiar with the investigation told ZDNet that Microsoft has not been able to confirm the Avast report.
 Until Google or Microsoft finish their investigations and decide what's their course of action, Avast recommended that users uninstall and remove the extensions from their browsers.

    有种说法说科技行业的未来在于德克萨斯州和佛罗里达州。厌倦了加州的压制性政策和高昂的房地产成本的技术工人和高管们，今年正大批迁往迈阿密和奥斯丁。这些城市正在建立占主导地位的人才基础，由于流行病，这种基础将持续数年。这种叙述是错误的。
 当故事放在新的LinkedIn数据旁边时，这个故事就崩溃了。该数据显示了2020年技术工人的实际流动方向。今年技术移民的主要受益者是威斯康星州麦迪逊市等不太喧嚣的城市。弗吉尼亚州里士满;和加利福尼亚萨克拉曼多。这些地方的新闻报道不多，但它们吸引技术人才的速度比去年高得多。相反，奥斯汀在吸引技术人员方面的步伐更慢。
 大技术公司（Big Technology）在这里首次发布了最新的LinkedIn数据，该数据检查了美国数十万名技术工人。该数据细分了他们进入城市与迁出城市的比率，LinkedIn称之为流入/流出比率。数据范围从4月到10月，将2020年与2019年进行比较。该数据涵盖了由于大流行而离开城市的核心月份。
 该国最大的技术移民增加发生在麦迪逊。去年，该市每增加一名技术工人，他们就会获得1.02名技术工人，而现在，该市的人数增加了1.77名，增长了74％。与此同时，萨克拉曼多和里士满都在大流行之前失去了技术人员，并扭转了局面。萨克拉曼多为去年离开的每一名技术工作者增加的比例仅为0.87，现在增加了1.02。里士满去年增加了0.95，今年增加了1.06。中西部的其他城市，包括明尼苏达州的明尼阿波利斯市和俄亥俄州的克利夫兰市，都大大降低了科技工作者离职的速度。
  LinkedIn首席经济学家Karin Kimbrough告诉Big Technology，这些技术人才的转移可能会激发经验丰富的社区中的技术和创业活动，从而带来持久的经济利益。她说：“我完全可以想象，拥有这种技能的人如果决定留在所谓的第二城市或非传统的技术中心，那么他们最终可能会剥离出自己的东西来制造自己的东西。”
 就奥斯丁而言，它并没有经历由大流行引起的科技工作者激增。去年，奥斯汀每离开一个人，就会获得2.06名技术人员。现在下降到1.84，下降了10.78％。尽管奥斯丁今年仍在吸引科技工作者，但关于2020年是科技工作者迁徙的分水岭的说法是一个神话。 
迈阿密是试图涂膏下一个硅谷的人们中另一个喧嚣的城市。风险投资家基思·拉博伊斯（Keith Rabois）上周表示：“如果您是企业家，现在在迈阿密进行风险投资可能比在CA容易。”但这可能是因为风险资本的供应量超过了技术人才的需求。据LinkedIn称，今年迈阿密的科技工作者的增长速度比去年快，但幅度却不大，增长了3％。
   现在来看丑陋的东西。美国仅有的两个失去技术工人的城市是纽约市和旧金山。纽约的流入/流出比率变化为-20％。旧金山的是-35％。为了说明这一点，旧金山以前每剩下100名工人就获得148名工人。现在仅增长96。这些急剧下降可能只是暂时的，因为这些城市可能在大流行后反弹。
 金布拉夫说：“有些人已经搬家并将留在新的地方，有些人可能愿意在几年后回到旧金山或纽约。” “我不给这些城市打电话。他们一定会回来的。”
 同时，西雅图在大流行期间一直保持着新进技术工人数量的增长。在大流行之前，该市为失去的每一名工作人员增加了2.52名技术人员，并且该比率一直保持在2以上。我想知道这是否意味着西雅图正在成为美国的新技术之都，以为亚马逊和微软已经成为上升可能与此有关。但是金布拉（Kimbrough）仔细研究了LinkedIn的数据以及其他房屋数据，并为这一想法注入了冷水。她说：“我们认为这些举措中的许多可能是短期的。” “我们预计，当办公室重新开放时，技术人才会重返湾区等传统技术中心。”换句话说，当心叙述。
        超过150位大技术读者完成了本月的读者调查，而我从您那里学到了很多。非常感谢您的周到答复；我读每一本书。我最喜欢的反馈之一是在“我们可以做得更好”框中，在那儿，经过一些建设性的批评，一位读者将Big Technology与HBO的《 Last Week Tonight》进行了比较：“不是头版新闻，而且非常及时（实时） ，但是深入探讨了一个重要的话题，有些人可能没有想到。”这正是我的目标。

  本月初，我主张在科技行业和媒体之间进行对话，并警告说，该行业新生的“直接”运动（建设性的使记者偏向于自己的出版物的做法）没有建设性。好吧，《大技术》读者和技术传播策略师Will Nevius通过电子邮件发送了反对意见。并且本着对话的精神，在他的允许下，我在这里印出他的答复。
 技术报道的基调是问责制驱动的（委婉的说法是对技术卑鄙的人，医管局），这可能是一件好事！关于技术在我们的生活中的角色，需要进行一些讨论。但是，很难说技术媒体的报道对行业至关重要，而且关注的是FANG。
 那么这对我们其他人意味着什么呢？与其他行业（即医疗保健）不同，没有贸易新闻。例如，生物技术领域有大量对分子生物学，新平台等感兴趣的出版物...化学博士学位的记者等。 
好消息？公司对谁对他们的业务至关重要（投资者，潜在招聘人员，客户等）比以往任何时候都了解得更好，并且工具比以往任何时候都更容易找到他们。宇宙是可知的，它有成百上千的人。当然，在NYT网站的首页上很棒，但是...发给200个人的密件抄送电子邮件的工作量可能要少得多，而且效率可能会大大提高。
 我认为技术交流的班级在说的是...有时候，技术媒体不值得麻烦。而且我认为这通常很公平。
  在您允许的情况下，我会打印周到的异议。如果您对未来的故事甚至是问题有这样的想法和建议，请随时回复，我们会尽力将其包括在内。

    There’s a narrative that the tech industry’s future lies in Texas and Florida. That tech workers and executives — sick of California’s oppressive policies and sky-high real estate costs — are moving en masse to Miami and Austin this year. That these cities are building dominant talent foundations that will persist for years due to the pandemic. That narrative is wrong.
 The story crumbles when placed next to new LinkedIn data showing where tech workers are actually moving in 2020. The key beneficiaries of this year’s tech migration are less buzzy cities like Madison, Wisconsin; Richmond, Virginia; and Sacramento, California. These places don’t get much play in the news, but they’re attracting tech talent at significantly higher rates than they were last year. Austin, conversely, is gaining tech workers more slowly.
 The new LinkedIn data, which  Big Technology is first publishing here, examines several hundred thousand tech workers in the U.S. It breaks down the ratio at which they’re moving into a city vs. moving out, something LinkedIn calls the inflow/outflow ratio. The data ranges from April to October, comparing 2020 with 2019. It encompasses the core months people left their cities due to the pandemic.
 The country’s biggest tech migration increase took place in Madison. The city was gaining 1.02 tech workers for each one that left last year, and it’s now gaining 1.77, a 74% jump. Sacramento and Richmond, meanwhile, were losing tech workers before the pandemic and have turned it around. Sacramento was adding a fraction of a tech worker — 0.87 — for each one that left last year, and now it’s adding 1.02. Richmond was adding 0.95 last year, and it’s adding 1.06 this year. Other Midwest cities, including Minneapolis, Minnesota, and Cleveland, Ohio, have significantly reduced the rate at which tech workers were leaving their cities.
  These tech talent shifts could invigorate tech and startup activity in the communities experiencing the gains, leading to lasting economic benefit, LinkedIn chief economist Karin Kimbrough told  Big Technology. “I could totally envision that people who have that skill, if they decide to remain in what might call the second cities or nontraditional tech hubs, they eventually could spin off into something where they build their own thing,” she said.
 Austin, for its part, is not experiencing a pandemic-induced tech worker surge. Last year, Austin was gaining 2.06 tech workers for every one that left; now it’s down to 1.84, a drop of 10.78%. Though Austin is still gaining tech workers this year, the notion that 2020 was a watershed year for tech workers moving there is a myth.
 Miami is another buzzy city among people trying to anoint the next Silicon Valley. “If you’re an entrepreneur, it is probably easier to raise a VC round in Miami right now than CA,”  said venture capitalist Keith Rabois last week. But that might be because the supply of VCs outweighs the demand from tech talent. Miami is gaining tech workers faster this year than last but not dramatically, an increase of 3%, according to LinkedIn.
   Now on to the ugly stuff. The only two cities in the country losing tech workers that previously were gaining them are New York City and San Francisco. New York’s change in inflow/outflow ratio is -20%. San Francisco’s is -35%. To put that in context, San Francisco was previously gaining 148 workers for every 100 that left; now it’s gaining only 96. These steep drops may only be temporary, however, as these cities will likely bounce back post-pandemic.
 “There are some people who have moved and are going to stay in their new place and some who might be willing to come back in a couple of years to San Francisco or New York,” Kimbrough said. “I'm not calling time on these cities. They will definitely come back.”
 Seattle, meanwhile, has kept up its surge of incoming tech workers during the pandemic. The city was adding 2.52 tech workers for each one it was losing before the pandemic, and it’s kept the ratio well above 2. I wondered if this meant Seattle was on its way to becoming the new tech capital of the U.S., thinking Amazon and Microsoft’s ascents might have something to do with it. But Kimbrough looked at LinkedIn’s data along with other housing data and poured cold water on the idea. “We believe a lot of these moves are likely short-term,” she said. “We expect to see tech talent returning to legacy tech hubs like the Bay Area when offices reopen.” In other words, beware the narrative.
        More than 150  Big Technology readers completed this month’s reader survey, and I learned a ton from you. Thanks so much for your thoughtful responses; I read every one. One of my favorites bits of feedback came in the “What can we do better” box, where, after some constructive criticism, one reader compared  Big Technology to HBO’s  Last Week Tonight: “Not the front page story and extremely timely (real time), but instead in depth about an important topic some people may not be thinking about.” That’s exactly what I’m aiming for.
  Big Technology readers generally like links to related reading underneath the main articles. So I will add more of those moving forward.
 On format, nearly 50% of  Big Technology readers said the current weekly article format is their most preferred, a plurality. So we’ll stick with that.
 Tech business and regulation news are the two forms of news  Big Technology readers are most interested in. That will be the main focus next year, and it’s good timing with antitrust lawsuits and tech giant fights like Apple vs. Facebook on the horizon.
 Two-thirds of  Big Technology readers said they’d be willing to pay to subscribe, which was heartening, and I appreciate it. Next year, I will turn on some form of subscription, with the details yet to be worked out. There will always be a free component to this newsletter.
 There were so many thoughtful replies in the open feedback section. These responses help me understand who I’m writing for and what you’re interested in. It will make this newsletter better. Thank you.
 Thanks again to everyone who completed the survey. I’ll leave the form open until January 1, 2021, in case anyone has any last thoughts. You can find it here:  https://forms.gle/dy3JDrTwrATrbpN48.
  Earlier this month, I argued for dialogue between the  tech industry and the press and warned that the industry’s nascent “go direct” movement — which sidelines journalists in favor of their own publications — isn’t constructive. Well,  Big Technology reader and tech communications strategist  Will Nevius emailed with a counterpoint. And in the spirit of dialogue, and with his permission, I’m printing his response here.
 The tone in tech coverage is accountability driven (a euphemism for mean to tech, ha) and that's probably a good thing! There's a needed conversation about the role of tech in our lives.... but it's difficult to argue tech media coverage is anything but critical of the industry and focused on FANG.
 So what does that mean for the rest of us? Unlike other industries (ie, healthcare), there is no trade press. For example, biotech has tons of publications interested in molecular biology, new platforms, etc...reporters with phd in chem etc.
 The good news? Companies have a better understanding than ever before of who matters to their biz (investors, potential recruits, customers, etc) and the tools are better than ever to reach them. The universe is knowable — it's hundreds or maybe thousands of people. Sure, being on the front page of the NYT is great but... a bcc email to 200 people is probably a lot less work and maybe significantly more effective.
 I think what the tech chattering class is saying is... sometimes tech media isn't worth the hassle. And i think that's often fair.
  I’ll print thoughtful dissent as it comes in, with your permission. If you have thoughts and pushback like this on future stories or even a question, feel free to reply, and I’ll do my best to include it.

总部位于加州帕洛阿尔托的云数据管理初创公司Rubrik的市值超过30亿美元，该公司已从西雅图初创公司Igneous收购了技术和IP资产，该公司上个月裁员。
 Igneous的平台由Isilon Systems和NetApp的资深人士于2013年创立，可为云或本地的非结构化数据或不容易分类的信息提供可视性和存储。
 该公司在11月份裁员人数不详，当时雇用了69名员工。现在，LinkedIn上列出的员工不到40名。迄今为止，Igneous已筹集了7000万美元。
  Rubrik在2019年1月以33亿美元的估值筹集了2.61亿美元。这家成立6年的公司帮助公司跨各种平台备份和保护其数据。
 Rubrik工程高级副总裁Vinod Marur在一份声明中表示：“将Igneous已构建的创新技术与Rubrik平台的强大功能相结合，将为我们的客户带来一流的非结构化数据管理解决方案。” “我们很高兴将Igneous团队带入Rubrik系列，使我们的客户能够在降低风险和优化IT资源利用率的同时实现其非结构化数据的价值。”
 Igneous由联合创始人兼前首席执行官Kiran Bhageshpur领导。根据一篇博客文章，他在6月离开了CEO职位之后，``有机会从我担任Igneous的首席执行官的角色中退缩''。据他的LinkedIn称，巴格什普尔（Bhageshpur）也于11月离开董事会，他正在“延长休息时间”。
 F5 Networks和Palo Alto Networks的前高管Dean Darwin接任首席执行官兼总裁，但他在11月卸任该职位。 
泰勒·索珀（Taylor Soper）是GeekWire的执行编辑，负责协调新闻编辑室，计划报道范围和编辑故事。 他是俄勒冈州波特兰市人，毕业于华盛顿大学，此前曾是GeekWire的一名记者，报道的领域包括创业公司和体育技术。 跟随他@taylor_soper并发送电子邮件至taylor@geekwire.com。
note_en:
Rubrik, a Palo Alto, Calif-based cloud data management startup valued at more than $3 billion, has acquired technology and IP assets from  Igneous, a Seattle startup that laid off staff last month.
 Founded in 2013 by veterans of Isilon Systems and NetApp, Igneous’ platform provides visibility and storage for unstructured data, or information that isn’t easily categorized, both in the cloud or on-premise.
 The company went through  an undisclosed number of layoffs in November and employed 69 people at the time; there are now less than 40 employees listed on LinkedIn. Igneous has raised $70 million to date.
  Rubrik  raised $261 million at a $3.3 billion valuation in January 2019. The 6-year-old company helps companies backup and protect their data across various platforms.
 “Combining the innovative technology that Igneous has built with the power and breadth of Rubrik’s platform will bring the best in class solution for unstructured data management to our customers,” Vinod Marur, SVP of engineering at Rubrik, said in a  statement. “We are very excited to bring the Igneous team into the Rubrik family and enable our customers to realize the value of their unstructured data while reducing risk and optimizing IT resource utilization.”
 Igneous was led by co-founder and former CEO  Kiran Bhageshpur. He left the CEO role in June after “the opportunity to step back from my role as the CEO of Igneous presented itself,”  according to a blog post. Bhageshpur, who also left the board in November, is “taking a very extended break,” according to his LinkedIn.
 Dean Darwin, a former exec at F5 Networks and Palo Alto Networks, took over as CEO and president but he left that role in November, according to his LinkedIn.
  Taylor Soper is GeekWire's managing editor, responsible for coordinating the newsroom, planning coverage, and editing stories. A native of Portland, Ore., and graduate of the University of Washington, he was previously a GeekWire staff reporter, covering beats including startups and sports technology. Follow him  @taylor_soper and email  taylor@geekwire.com.

title_en: Rubrik, a data management startup last valued at $3.3B in 2019, acquires tech and IP assets from Igneous, which provides unstructured data management services

 美国证券交易委员会（Securities and Exchange Commission）周四指控罗宾汉（Robinhood）欺骗客户有关股票交易应用程序如何赚钱的行为，并且未能提供承诺的最佳交易执行方式。
 Robinhood同意支付6500万美元的民事罚款，但不承认或否认SEC的调查结果。该公司的律师表示，这种做法并不能反映当今的``知更鸟身份''。
  最终有计划公开上市的硅谷初创公司在2020年筹集了超过10亿美元的资金，将Robinhood的估值提升至117亿美元。
 ＆＃34;在2015年至2018年末之间，Robinhood在描述其如何赚钱时（即从贸易公司以换取Robinhood的付款），在其客户交流中（包括其网站上的FAQ页面）做出了误导性陈述和遗漏，涉及其最大的收入来源将其客户订单发送给这些公司以执行，也称为“订单流支付”， SEC说。

千禧一代最喜欢的交易应用最出名的是率先推出了“免佣金交易”。 Robinhood和在线经纪业的其他部门都依靠所谓的订单流支付作为利润引擎来代替佣金。
 为华尔街公司的订单流付款是有争议的，但是大多数电子经纪人都遵循法律惯例。对于Robinhood来说，这是最大的收入来源。根据美国证券交易委员会的文件，Robinhood在第二季度获得了1.8亿美元的交易付款。
  SEC的命令发现，即使考虑了不支付佣金而节省下来的费用，Robinhood的交易价格也较低，使客户损失了3410万美元。

 由于Covid-19大流行带来的前所未有的市场动荡，Robinhood在2020年实现了创纪录的增长。 Robinhood在2020年前四个月发布了创纪录的300万新客户。

Robinhood发言人补充说：＆＃34;我们在与客户的沟通中完全透明地了解我们当前的收入流，显着改善了我们的最佳执行流程，并与其他做市商建立了联系，以提高执行质量。  SEC的指控发生在马萨诸塞州的监管机构提出指控的第二天，该指控指责该交易应用程序对经验不足的投资者实施掠夺性营销。  该投诉引用了罗宾汉的激进策略以吸引缺乏经验的投资者，使用游戏化策略操纵客户以及未能防止交易平台频繁停机和中断。  订阅CNBC PRO以获得独家见解和分析，以及来自世界各地的实时工作日节目。
note_en:
The SEC charged Robinhood with deceiving customers about how the stock trading app makes money and failing to deliver the promised best execution of trades.
 Robinhood agreed to pay a $65 million civil penalty, without admitting or denying SEC's findings.
 The Securities and Exchange Commission on Thursday charged Robinhood with deceiving customers about how the stock trading app makes money and failing to deliver the promised best execution of trades.
 Robinhood agreed to pay a $65 million civil penalty, without admitting or denying SEC's findings. A lawyer for the company said the practices "do not reflect Robinhood today."
  The Silicon Valley start-up, which has eventual plans to go public, has raised more than $1 billion in funding in 2020, lifting Robinhood's valuation to $11.7 billion.
 "Between 2015 and late 2018, Robinhood made misleading statements and omissions in customer communications, including in FAQ pages on its website, about its largest revenue source when describing how it made money — namely, payments from trading firms in exchange for Robinhood sending its customer orders to those firms for execution, also known as 'payment for order flow,'" the SEC said.
 "One of Robinhood's selling points to customers was that trading was 'commission free,' but due in large part to its unusually high payment for order flow rates, Robinhood customers' orders were executed at prices that were inferior to other brokers' prices," the statement added.
 The millennial-favored trading app is best known for pioneering the "commission-free trading." Robinhood, and the rest of the online brokerage industry, rely on what's known as payment for order flow as their profit engine in lieu of commissions.
 Taking payments for order flow from Wall Street firms is a controversial, but legal practice done by most electronic brokers. For Robinhood, it's the  biggest revenue source. Robinhood received $180 million in payments for trades in the second quarter, according to an SEC filing.
  The SEC order found that Robinhood provided inferior trade prices that cost customers $34.1 million, even after considering the savings from not paying a commission.
 "Robinhood provided misleading information to customers about the true costs of choosing to trade with the firm," said Stephanie Avakian, director of the SEC's Enforcement Division.  "Brokerage firms cannot mislead customers about order execution quality."
 Market makers such as Citadel Securities or Virtu pay e-brokers like Robinhood for the right to execute customer trades. The broker is then paid a small fee for the shares that are routed, which can add up to millions when customers trade as actively as they have this year.
 Robinhood has experienced record growth in 2020 due to the unprecedented market volatility from the Covid-19 pandemic. Robinhood posted a record 3 million new customers in the first four months of 2020.
 "The settlement relates to historical practices that do not reflect Robinhood today," said Dan Gallagher, Robinhood's chief legal officer at Robinhood. "We recognize the responsibility that comes with having helped millions of investors make their first investments, and we're committed to continuing to evolve Robinhood as we grow to meet our customers' needs."
 A Robinhood spokesperson added:  "We are fully transparent in our communications with customers about our current revenue streams, have significantly improved our best execution processes, and have established relationships with additional market makers to improve execution quality."
 The SEC's charges came a day after  Massachusetts regulators filed a complaint accusing the trading app of predatory marketing on inexperienced investors.
 The complaint cites Robinhood's "aggressive tactics to attract inexperienced investors, its use of gamification strategies to manipulate customers, and its failure to prevent frequent outages and disruptions on its trading platform."

华盛顿（路透社）-两名知情人士周四告诉路透社，美国定于周五将包括中国最大的芯片制造商中芯国际在内的数十家中国公司加入交易黑名单。
  此举此前未见报道，被视为唐纳德·特朗普总统为巩固其在中国的强硬遗产所做的最新努力。它配备民主党总统当选人拜登之前的几个星期设置坐办公室1月20日。
 总体而言，预计美国将在所谓的实体列表中增加约80家公司和关联公司，几乎所有这些公司和关联公司都是中国公司。
 预计美国商务部的任命将列出一些华盛顿表示与中国军方有联系的中国公司，包括一些帮助其在南中国海建造和军事化人工岛的公司，以及一些涉嫌侵犯人权的公司。消息人士说。
  特朗普政府经常使用实体名单（目前包括超过275家在中国的公司和分支机构）来冲击中国的关键行业。
 其中包括电信设备巨头华为技术有限公司和150家分支机构，以及中兴通讯因违反制裁规定而行事，以及监控摄像头制造商海康威视因压制中国维吾尔族而受到打击。
   9月，商务部命令该公司的某些设备的供应商得出结论认为，向其供应的设备可用于军事目的存在“不可接受的风险”，因此该公司向其申请了出口许可证。 
中芯国际，商务部和中国驻华盛顿使馆没有立即回应置评请求。
 上个月，国防部将该公司列入了所谓的中国军事公司的黑名单，有效地禁止了美国投资者从明年初开始购买其股票。
   实体名单的指定将迫使中芯国际在美国供应商向其发送关键商品之前向商务部寻求特殊许可，这是美国政府为遏制其使用先进的美国芯片制造技术而进行的竞标的一部分。
 消息人士称，预计商务部还将把众多中芯国际的附属公司加入实体名单。
  中芯国际是中国最大的芯片制造商，但落后于该行业的市场领导者台积电。它寻求建立可以与台积电竞争的计算机芯片制造厂。
 在过去的一年中，华盛顿与北京之间的联系变得越来越具有对抗性，因为全球前两个经济体就北京处理冠状病毒爆发，香港实施国家安全法以及南中国海紧张局势加剧而争执不下。
note_en:
WASHINGTON (Reuters) - The United States is set to add dozens of Chinese companies, including the country’s top chipmaker SMIC, to a trade blacklist on Friday, two people familiar with the matter told Reuters on Thursday.
  The move, which has not previously been reported, is seen as the latest in President Donald Trump’s effort to cement his tough-on-China legacy. It comes just weeks before Democratic President-elect Joe Biden is set to take office on Jan. 20.
 In total, the United States is expected to add around 80 additional companies and affiliates to the so-called entity list, nearly all of them Chinese.
 The designations by the Commerce Department are expected to name some Chinese companies that Washington says have ties to the Chinese military, including some helping it construct and militarize artificial islands in the South China Sea, as well as some involved in alleged human rights violations, the sources said.
  The Trump administration has often used the entity list - which now includes more than 275 China-based firms and affiliates - to hit key Chinese industries.
 These include telecoms equipment giants Huawei Technologies Co and 150 affiliates, and ZTE Corp over sanction violations, as well as surveillance camera maker Hikvision over suppression of China’s Uighur minority.
   In September, the Commerce Department mandated that suppliers of certain equipment to the company apply for export licenses after concluding there was an “unacceptable risk” that equipment supplied to it could be used for military purposes.
 SMIC, the Commerce Department and the Chinese Embassy in Washington did not immediately respond to requests for comment Thursday.
 Last month, the Defense Department added the company to a blacklist of alleged Chinese military companies, effectively banning U.S. investors from buying its shares starting late next year.
   The entity list designation would force SMIC to seek a special license from the Commerce Department before a U.S supplier could send it key goods, part of a bid by the administration to curb its access to sophisticated U.S. chipmaking technology.
 Commerce is also expected to add numerous SMIC-affiliated companies to the entity list, the sources said.
  SMIC is the largest Chinese chip manufacturer but trails Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co, the industry’s market leader. It has sought to build out foundries for the manufacture of computer chips that can compete with TSMC.
 Ties between Washington and Beijing have grown increasingly antagonistic over the past year as the world’s top two economies sparred over Beijing’s handling of the coronavirus outbreak, imposition of a national security law in Hong Kong and rising tensions in the South China Sea.
title: 资料来源：美国周五将向包括中芯国际在内的约80家中国公司和分支机构增加贸易黑名单
title_en: Sources: US to add ~80 more Chinese companies and affiliates, including SMIC, to the trade blacklist on Friday

note:
媒体与市场营销这家领先的流媒体播放器制造商已采取了苛刻的谈判策略，以提供HBO Max和Peacock等服务
   在圣诞节电影《 1984年奇妙的女人》（Wonder Woman 1984）在圣诞节上映之前的不到两周，在电影院和美国的客厅里，美国最大的流媒体播放器都没有与之合作。
  Roku ROKU 1.12％尚未与WarnerMedia达成协议，该协议将使Roku ROKU将其备受期待的大型预算眼镜（或HBO Max上出现的其他任何内容）传送给数百万客户。 WarnerMedia财务状况不佳。
  长期以来，Roku被视为发明流媒体播放器的草率创业公司。现在，这是一个400亿美元的巨头，越来越难以承受其重量。
  作为美国流媒体视频应用程序的领先发行商，Roku今年在娱乐行业的巨头中进行了几场战斗，这得益于其在市场上的主导地位和不断飙升的估值。
  本周，Roku在同意财务条款后结束了与WarnerMedia的八个月对峙。 1984年的《神力女超人》（Wonder Woman 1984）成为双方的动力。
  Roku今年还与Fox纠缠不清，该纠纷有可能使数以百万计的流媒体观众失去超级碗，而Comcast NBCUniversal于今年夏天推出了Peacock流媒体。 
熟悉讨论的人士说，这些斗争的核心是如何分散视频流的问题。该部门在今年因剪断电线和因冠状病毒大流行而留在家中而蓬勃发展，掀起了争夺谁来统治娱乐发行新秩序的争夺战。
  消费者可以以多种方式访问​​流应用程序，最常见的是通过Roku，Apple Apple TV和Amazon.com FireTV等机顶设备。他们还可以获得插入屏幕的小“棒”。现在，许多新电视都带有Roku或其他提供商提供的预设操作软件，可对流媒体应用程序进行内置访问。
  根据研究公司Parks Associates的调查，总部位于加利福尼亚州圣何塞的Roku在上述类别中名列前茅，在美国市场中占有38％的份额。
  在美国，亚马逊紧随Roku之后，而苹果，Alphabet，谷歌和三星则紧随其后。
    自2017年上市以来，Roku的市值增长了30倍，超过400亿美元，超过了ViacomCBS和Fox Corp.。其市值超过Discovery AMC Networks和Lions Gate Entertainment的总和。自3月中旬以来，随着家庭用户流式传输更多视频，其份额已增长260％。
  Roku首席执行官兼创始人Anthony Wood表示：“我们经常被行业低估。”他将公司命名为日语中的“六”，因为这是他创办的第六家公司。他被某些员工形容为冷漠而难以理解，他对传统媒体公司不耐烦，这些公司对流媒体时代的变化感到不满。
  尽管Roku建立了销售流媒体设备的业务，但如今，它几乎没有从该硬件上获利，而是专注于在其携带的流媒体应用程序中销售广告。 Roku在其Channel Store中拥有超过10,000个免费和付费应用程序。根据与应用所有者的安排，通常可获得广告空间的百分比。广告是Roku去年11亿美元收入的最大贡献。 Roku通常还会从合作伙伴那里获得一定比例的订阅收入。 
Roku希望从应用程序中获得更大的广告收入，并通过其Roku Channel（其提供电影和电视节目的应用程序）本身将自己定位为程序员。娱乐公司通常不急于将任何广告空间或内容交给Roku。
  伍德先生说，一些紧张的谈判是自然的，因为传统媒体公司承受着适应流媒体娱乐的压力。他说：“没有人喜欢改变，电视行业正在发生着巨大的变化和破坏。” “这将创造新的赢家和输家，这很难。”
  一些媒体高管说，Roku帮助扩大了业务，与该公司的往来很顺利。有人说，要让流媒体应用程序在亚马逊的FireTV上进行交易，他们面临更大的挑战。
  对其他人来说，Roku的要求过高。流媒体行业的战斗呼应了那些媒体公司多年来与有线电视提供商就承载其电视网络的条款进行的斗争。争议的问题有所不同，但主题是相同的，内容制作者与发行人争执不休。
  莫菲特·内森森（Moffett Nathanson）的分析师迈克尔·内森森（Michael Nathanson）谈到Roku时表示：“他们已经取代了有线运营商，成为了看门人。 “那些家伙也很狠。”
  Roku和HBO Max有很多达成交易的动机；如果Roku设备上没有提供“神奇女侠”，则双方都有风险。 WarnerMedia计划将其用于该电影的模型（将在影院上映，但也将在美国以HBO Max的价格在一个月内使用）应用于整部2021年电影。
    Roku与WarnerMedia和其他公司进行谈判的主要要点是为Roku频道进行编程，该频道与Roku服务中的竞争电视应用程序并排。一位知情人士说，WarnerMedia不想共享Roku频道的任何内容。 
Roku最初向媒体公司付款以许可该频道的内容。知情人士说，但最近，Roku要求媒体公司提供程序设计，以作为携带其应用程序的代价。如果媒体公司以其他方式（如广告或订阅费）向Roku提供足够的补偿，则该公司会放宽对Roku Channel节目的要求。
  一位知情人士说，根据两家公司的协议，华纳媒体没有为Roku频道提供内容。知情人士说，Roku还敦促在即将发布的HBO Max版本中投放广告，以分享广告空间。在分销交易中，双方通常都会有所收获。
  一位Roku高管在周三的新闻稿中称HBO Max交易是互惠互利的协议。
  伍德先生说，与传统的有线电视谈判相比，Roku在媒体交易方面的要求不高，而且考虑到该公司在流媒体发行方面的投资。 “我们花费了数亿美元。我们不是免费的。”他说。 “我的意思是，这对所有人都有好处，除了少数几家老牌公司，因为它们只需要改变。”
  伍德拥有近59亿美元的Roku股票，并拥有68％的投票权，他有破坏娱乐业务的历史。他发明了数字录像机以跟上“星际迷航”的风潮，并于1997年创立了ReplayTV Inc.。随后，他看到快速追随者TiVo上市并家喻户晓。
    伍德先生随后发明了流媒体播放器，并于2002年创立了Roku。该公司最早的投资者，包括风险投资公司和老牌媒体公司，在他看来，他是一位雄心勃勃的创始人，他知道获得第二名的感觉，并且从不愿意这样做。再次体验。
  Netflix是Roku上第一个可用的应用程序，并帮助孵化了业务。伍德先生在Netflix工作了不到一年，而同时经营Roku。 
这位54岁的高个子老人在公共场合轻声细语，被商业伙伴和员工形容为私下里的边缘人。他以运动工作服或吊带裤上班而闻名，他驾驶全电动雪佛兰Bolt并将鸡作为宠物饲养。伍德先生的父母从英国移民，他是英国媒体的粉丝，从阿加莎·克里斯蒂（Agatha Christie）小说到英国广播公司BBC和ITV PLC的合资企业Britbox流媒体服务。
  前员工说，Roku继承了Netflix繁琐的工作场所文化的一些特征，例如倾向于快速雇用和解雇。伍德先生成立了“娱乐部”，为Roku制作装饰和附加内容。他最喜欢的是水族馆屏幕保护程序。
  伍德先生将内容谈判委托给受信任的代理人斯科特·罗森伯格（Scott Rosenberg），但定下了基调。 Roku在今年夏天与Comcast的NBCUniversal对抗，因为它拥有7月推出的孔雀流媒体服务。
  知情人士说，Roku希望孔雀有30％的广告时间可用，但孔雀高管不想放弃任何广告空间，也不想为Roku频道提供有价值的节目。
  当双方未能在8月31日的最后期限之前达成协议时，Roku几乎删除了数十个NBCUniversal应用程序。在数周后达成的交易中，NBCUniversal向Roku提供了一些广告资源和大量的库编程，包括“ Magnum P.I.”。一位与会谈的知情人士说，这部影片还包括《 Xena：战士公主》以及约45部电影。 Roku没有获得NBCUniversal的几处最著名的财产的权利，例如“办公室”或“公园和娱乐场所”。孔雀在接下来的几个月里还许可了更多的游戏，但尚未透露。
    知情人士说，康卡斯特（Comcast）首席执行官布莱恩·罗伯茨（Brian Roberts）渴望进入Roku的地盘。据《华尔街日报》报道，康卡斯特正在与沃尔玛进行谈判，以开发和分发可以运行康卡斯特软件并成为流媒体应用中心的智能电视。根据两家公司讨论过的条款，沃尔玛将获得经常性收入的一部分，而电视机则可能带有沃尔玛品牌。
  知情人士说，沃尔玛之所以可能转向康卡斯特，部分原因是它与Roku有关的自身问题。一位知情人士说，沃尔玛帮助Roku建立了业务，成为Roku的最大分销商，也是加入Roku软件的自有品牌电视机的合作伙伴。随着时间的流逝，沃尔玛对Roku的财务状况感到沮丧。 
沃尔玛美国公司电子副总裁赖安·彼得森（Ryan Peterson）表示，该公司没有与现有或潜在合作伙伴分享其讨论的详细信息。
  在2月2日的超级碗比赛之前，Roku在1月份与Fox进行了对抗。有一天，Roku威胁要放弃包括Fox Sports在内的Fox的应用程序，并宣传人们观看游戏的其他方式，这是有线电视纠纷中经常使用的一种策略。
    赛前周五，两家公司达成了交易。一位知情人士说，福克斯并未在交易中提供任何广告资源或为Roku频道提供内容，但同意将广告资源出售给Roku。当时，福克斯拥有Roku大约5％的股份，此后一直出售。福克斯首席执行官拉克兰·默多克（Lachlan Murdoch）在财报电话会议上表示，此次讨论“非常专业”，并对伍德先生及其管理团队为Roku的成长表示赞赏。
    去年，Roku参加了与拥有Lifetime和A＆amp; E等频道的A + E Inc.的谈判，要求获得将程序员的库放到Roku频道上并从这些节目中获得50％广告收入的权利。一个熟悉谈判的人。 A + E抵制并提供较少的广告收入。这位知情人士说，它仍然必须赋予Roku某些其最重要节目的约600集的权利，包括“ Dog the Bounty Hunter”，“ Hoarders”，“ First 48”和“ I Survived”。
  “ Roku是该领域的创新领导者和出色的合作伙伴，” A + E Networks发言人表示。
  Roku还面临着一家老牌有线电视公司的低调运输纠纷。与Charter Communications的合同到期后，Roku于12月12日从其频道商店中删除了Charter的Spectrum应用，该应用使Charter订户可以通过Roku设备观看有线电视。此举使新用户无法安装Spectrum应用，但是不会影响现有用户。
  与媒体公司的僵局并没有减慢Roku的增长。尽管在冠状病毒大流行中，广告支出总体上大幅下降，但Roku的收入在第三季度猛增了73％。与去年同期相比，今年前9个月Roku播放的小时数增长了53％，达到417亿。 Roku在此期间新增了900万个新的活动帐户。 
Fox Networks集团前数字产品总裁，现在是媒体和科技投资者，David Wertheimer表示Roku有理由进取，因为流媒体电视是其全部业务，而不是大型企业集团的一部分。 亚马逊，谷歌和其他。  他说：“可以理解，他们坚持不懈。” “现实是他们一直在玩的游戏奏效了。”  出现在2020年12月18日，印刷版为“ Roku Battles Media Giants in Quest to Dominate Streaming”。
note_en:
Media & Marketing     The maker of the leading streaming-media player has taken up brash negotiating tactics over its terms to carry services like HBO Max and Peacock
   Less than two weeks before  the Christmas Day release of the movie “Wonder Woman 1984” in theaters and in American living rooms, the biggest streaming-media player in the U.S. didn’t have a deal to show it.
  Roku    ROKU  1.12%  had yet to reach an agreement with WarnerMedia that would let it stream the highly anticipated, big-budget spectacle—or anything else appearing on HBO Max—to its millions of customers. It had tough financial terms WarnerMedia wouldn’t meet.
  Roku was long viewed as the scrappy startup that invented the streaming-media player. Now, it is a $40 billion juggernaut increasingly comfortable throwing around its weight.
  The leading distributor of streaming-video apps in the U.S., Roku has waged several fights this year with giants of the entertainment industry, emboldened by its dominant market position and skyrocketing valuation.
  This week, Roku  ended its eight-month standoff with WarnerMedia after agreeing to financial terms. An impetus for both sides was “Wonder Woman 1984,” which will appear on HBO Max in the U.S. the same day it appears in theaters.
  Roku has also tangled this year with  Fox  , a dispute that threatened to black out the Super Bowl for millions of streaming viewers, and  Comcast  NBCUniversal, which launched the streamer Peacock this summer.
  At the heart of these battles, say people familiar with the discussions, is the question of how to divvy up the spoils of video streaming. The segment has boomed this year amid cord-cutting and people staying at home due to the coronavirus pandemic, setting off a scramble for who will reign in the new order of entertainment distribution.
  Consumers can get access to streaming apps in a variety of ways, most commonly through set-top devices like Roku,  Apple  Apple TV and  Amazon.com  FireTV. They can also get  small “sticks” that plug into their screens. And many new TVs now come with preset operating software, from Roku or other providers, that offers built-in access to streaming apps.
  Roku, based in San Jose, Calif., leads the pack across those categories with 38% of the total U.S. market, according to research firm Parks Associates, with some 46 million active accounts globally.
  Amazon is close on Roku’s heels in the U.S., while Apple,  Alphabet  Google and  Samsung are further behind.
    Since going public in 2017, Roku’s market value has expanded 30 times to more than $40 billion, surpassing those of  ViacomCBS  and Fox Corp. It is worth more than  Discovery   AMC Networks and  Lions Gate Entertainment  combined. Its shares are up 260% since mid-March, as at-home consumers stream more video.
  “We’re often underestimated by the industry,” said Anthony Wood, Roku’s chief executive and founder, who named the company after the Japanese word for “six” because it was the sixth company he started. Described as aloof and inscrutable by some employees, he’s impatient with legacy media companies that chafe at the streaming era’s changes.
  Although Roku built its business selling streaming devices, these days it barely makes any profit off that hardware, and instead focuses on  selling ads in the streaming apps it carries. Roku has more than 10,000 apps in its Channel Store, both free and paid. It generally gets a percentage of the ad space, based on an arrangement with the app’s owner. Ads were the greatest contributor to Roku’s $1.1 billion in revenue last year. Roku also generally gets a percentage of subscription revenue from partners.
    Roku wants a larger slice of the ad money flowing through apps and is positioning itself as a programmer in its own right with The Roku Channel, its own app that offers movies and TV shows. Entertainment companies often aren’t eager to give over any ad space or content to Roku.
  Mr. Wood said some tense negotiations are natural, because traditional media companies are under pressure to adapt to streamed entertainment. “No one likes to change, and there’s a huge amount of change happening and disruption happening in the TV industry,” he said. “That’s going to create new winners and losers, and that’s hard.”
  Some media executives say that Roku has helped expand their businesses and that dealings with the company have been smooth. Some say they’ve had greater challenges doing deals to have their streaming apps carried on  Amazon’s FireTV .
  To others, Roku is unreasonably demanding. The streaming industry’s battles echo those media companies have fought for years with cable providers over the terms for carrying their TV networks. The disputed issues are different, but the theme is the same, with content makers at loggerheads with distributors.
  “They’ve kind of replaced cable operators as gatekeepers,” Michael Nathanson, an analyst at Moffett Nathanson, said of Roku. “Those guys were pretty ruthless too.”
  Roku and HBO Max had plenty of incentive to reach a deal; there were risks for both sides if “Wonder Woman” wasn’t available on Roku devices. WarnerMedia plans to  apply the model it’s using for that movie—which will be released in theaters but will also be available for a month in the U.S. on HBO Max—to its entire 2021 movie slate.
    A primary sticking point in Roku’s negotiations with WarnerMedia and others has been programming for The Roku Channel, which sits alongside competing TV apps in Roku’s service. WarnerMedia didn’t want to share any content for The Roku Channel, said a person familiar with the matter.
  Roku initially paid media companies to license content for the channel. But lately, Roku has demanded programming from media companies as a toll for carrying their apps, people familiar with Roku negotiations said. If media companies provide enough compensation to Roku in other ways, such as ad or subscription money, the company relaxes its demands for Roku Channel programming.
  WarnerMedia didn’t supply content for The Roku Channel under the companies’ agreement, a person familiar with the terms said. Roku also pressed for a share of ad space in a coming version of HBO Max that will carry ads, said people familiar with the situation. In distribution deals, both sides typically come away with something.
  A Roku executive called the HBO Max deal a mutually beneficial agreement in a press release Wednesday.
  Mr. Wood said Roku’s requests in media deals are modest, compared with traditional cable television negotiations, and given the company’s investments in streaming distribution. “We spend hundreds of millions of dollars. We don’t do it for free,” he said. “I mean, it’s good for everyone except for maybe a few legacy companies, because they just have to change.”
  Mr. Wood, who holds nearly $5.9 billion in Roku stock and has 68% of its voting power, has a history of disrupting the entertainment business. He invented the digital video recorder to keep up with “Star Trek” episodes and founded ReplayTV Inc. in 1997. He then watched as fast follower TiVo went public and became a household name.
    Mr. Wood then invented the streaming-media player and founded Roku in 2002. The company’s earliest investors, including venture firms and old-guard media companies, saw in him an ambitious founder who knew what it was like to finish second and never wanted to experience that again.
  Netflix was the first app available on Roku and helped incubate the business. Mr. Wood worked at Netflix for less than a year while simultaneously running Roku.
  Soft-spoken in public, the stocky 54-year-old is described by business partners and employees as hard-edged in private. He’s known to sport overalls or suspenders to the office, drives an all-electric Chevrolet Bolt and has kept chickens as pets. Mr. Wood, whose parents immigrated from the U.K., is a fan of British media, from Agatha Christie novels to the Britbox streaming service, a joint venture of British broadcasters BBC and  ITV PLC.
  Former employees say Roku inherited some features of  Netflix’s rough-and-tumble workplace culture, such as a tendency to hire and fire quickly. Mr. Wood established a “Department of the Fun Department” to work on frills and add-ons for Roku. His favorite is an aquarium screen saver.
  Mr. Wood delegates content negotiations to a trusted deputy, Scott Rosenberg, but sets the tone. Roku faced off with Comcast’s NBCUniversal this summer over carrying its  Peacock streaming service, which launched in July.
  Roku wanted 30% of the ad time available in Peacock, but Peacock executives didn’t want to give over any ad space and didn’t want to supply valuable programming for the Roku Channel, people close to the talks said.
  When the sides failed to reach a deal by an Aug. 31 deadline, several dozen NBCUniversal apps were nearly removed from Roku. In the deal reached weeks later, NBCUniversal gave Roku some ad inventory and a substantial amount of library programming, including “Magnum P.I.” and “Xena: Warrior Princess,” as well as some 45 films, according to one of the people close to the talks. Roku didn’t get rights to several of NBCUniversal’s best-known properties, such as “The Office” or “Parks and Recreation.” Peacock licensed many more titles for the coming months, which haven’t yet been disclosed.
    Brian Roberts, chief executive of Comcast, is eager to move into Roku’s turf, people familiar with his thinking say. Comcast is in talks with  Walmart  to develop and distribute smart TVs that could run Comcast software and be a hub for streaming apps,  The Wall Street Journal reported. Under the terms the companies have discussed, Walmart would get a share of recurring revenue and the TV sets could carry Walmart branding.
  Walmart’s drift toward Comcast was possible, in part, because of its own issues with Roku, people familiar with the situation said. Walmart helped build Roku’s business, as its largest distributor and a partner on private-label TV sets that have Roku software baked in. Over time, Walmart grew frustrated at Roku’s financial terms, one of the people said.
  Ryan Peterson, vice president of electronics for Walmart U.S., said the company doesn’t share details of its discussions with current or potential partners.
  Roku butted heads with Fox in January, ahead of the Feb. 2 Super Bowl. For a day, Roku threatened to drop Fox’s apps, including Fox Sports, and publicized other ways that people could watch the game, a tactic often used in cable TV disputes.
    The companies reached a deal the Friday before the game. Fox didn’t give away any ad inventory or provide content for The Roku Channel in the deal, but did agree to sell ad inventory to Roku, said a person familiar with the talks. At the time, Fox owned a roughly 5% stake in Roku, which it has since sold. Fox Chief Executive Lachlan Murdoch said on an earnings call that the discussions were “very professional” and praised Mr. Wood and his management team for Roku’s growth.
    Last year, Roku showed up to negotiations with A+E Inc., which owns channels like Lifetime and A&E, asking for the right to put the programmer’s library on The Roku Channel and take 50% of ad revenue from those shows, said a person familiar with the talks. A+E resisted and provided a smaller amount of ad revenue. It still had to give Roku rights to some 600 episodes of some of its top shows, including “Dog the Bounty Hunter,” “Hoarders,” “First 48” and “I Survived,” the person said.
  “Roku is an innovative leader in the space and a great partner,” said an A+E Networks spokeswoman.
  Roku also faces a lower-profile carriage dispute with an old-school cable company. After a contract with  Charter Communications  expired, Roku removed Charter’s Spectrum app, which lets Charter subscribers watch cable TV through a Roku device, from its channel store on Dec. 12. The move made it impossible for new users to install the Spectrum app, but didn’t affect existing users.
  The standoffs with media companies haven’t slowed Roku’s growth. While advertising spending is down sharply overall in the coronavirus pandemic, Roku’s revenue surged 73% in the third quarter. Hours streamed on Roku grew 53% to 41.7 billion in the first nine months of the year, compared with the same period a year ago. Roku added 9 million new active accounts over that span.
  David Wertheimer, a former president of digital products at Fox Networks Group who is now a media and tech investor, said Roku has reason to be aggressive, since streaming TV is its entire business, rather than a portion of a larger conglomerate as it is for Amazon, Google and others.
  “It’s understandable that they’re sticking to their guns,” he said. “The reality is the game that they’ve been playing has worked.”
  Appeared in the December 18, 2020, print edition as 'Roku Battles Media Giants in Quest to Dominate Streaming.'
title: 随着收入的增长，Roku与媒体公司的谈判变得越来越有力，与Fox和WarnerMedia的斗争便证明了这一点。
title_en: As its revenue surges, Roku has become increasingly forceful in its negotiations with media companies, as evidenced by its fights with Fox and WarnerMedia

note:
Google计划关闭Android Things，这是专为智能家居设备设计的简化版Android。该操作系统从未真正起步，因此损失不算太大，但这是Google庞大的关闭项目墓地中的另一项内容。
 智能家居项目于2015年以Brillo的名称开始，旨在提供“物联网的基础操作系统”。 2016年，Google对Brillo进行了改造，并重新启动了Android Things计划，该计划同样可以在连接的扬声器，安全摄像头和路由器等产品上运行。通过依赖Android，该操作系统应该被开发人员所熟悉并且易于上手。
  然后什么也没发生。在2018年，一些使用基础操作系统的最初的智能扬声器和智能显示器问世了。似乎没有其他公司对此感兴趣，因为在2019年2月，谷歌宣布将“重新定位” Android Things，专门针对智能扬声器和智能显示器。
 大约两年后，Android Things即将关闭。从2021年1月5日开始，允许开发人员将更新推送到其设备的Android Things控制台将停止接受新的非商业项目。一年后，即2022年1月5日，“该控制台将被完全关闭，所有项目数据将被永久删除。”从本质上讲，这意味着开发人员有一年的时间来结束他们已经建立的所有Android Things项目。
 对于一个项目，这是一个光荣的结局，在过去的五年中，这个项目几乎没有引起任何注意，特别是随着智能家居设备开始迅速普及。
 如果您有兴趣，请参阅以下有关Android Things的故事，该故事讲述了它在The Verge上发布的所有四个标题：
note_en:
Google plans to  shut down Android Things, a stripped-down version of Android designed for smart home devices. The OS never really got off the ground, so this isn’t all that much of a loss, but it is yet another entry in Google’s  expansive graveyard of shut-down projects.
 The smart home project got its start in 2015 under the name Brillo, which was meant to provide the “underlying operating system for the internet of things.” In 2016, Google revamped Brillo and relaunched the initiative as Android Things, which was likewise meant to run on products like connected speakers, security cameras, and routers. By relying on Android, the OS was supposed to be familiar to developers and easy to get started with.
  Then nothing happened. In 2018, some initial smart speakers and smart displays came out using the underlying OS. It seems no other companies were interested, because in February 2019, Google announced it was “refocusing” Android Things to cater specifically to smart speakers and smart displays.
 Nearly two years later, and Android Things is now on track to be shut down. The Android Things Console, which lets developers push updates to their devices, will stop accepting new non-commercial projects starting January 5th, 2021. A year later, on January 5th, 2022, “the console will be turned down completely and all project data will be permanently deleted.” That essentially means developers have a year to wind down any Android Things projects they already have set up.
 It’s an inglorious ending for a project that, over five years, barely made a dent in the landscape, particularly as smart home devices began to boom in popularity.
 In case you’re interested, here’s the story of Android Things, told across all four headlines it made on  The Verge:
title: Google将于2022年1月5日关闭专为智能家居设备设计的Android精简版Android Things。
title_en: Google to shut down Android Things, a stripped-down version of Android designed for smart home devices, on January 5th, 2022

GitHub CEO Nat Friedman在今天的公告中写道：“没人喜欢Cookie横幅。” “但是饼干横幅到处都是！”
 当然，这样做的原因是由于美国的GDPR等法规以及欧盟的指令（赋予用户拒绝使用cookie的权利，从而降低了他们的在线隐私权）的要求。即使这些法规牢记了用户的最大利益，其结果仍然是您今天不断遇到的cookie条幅。
  “在GitHub，我们希望保护开发人员的隐私，并且发现cookie标语令人不快，因此我们决定寻求解决方案。简短搜索后，我们发现了一个：只是不使用任何非必要的Cookie。真的很简单，”弗里德曼写道。
 公平地说，对于像GitHub这样的服务，取消cookie可能比大多数网站（尤其是内容网站）要容易一些（是的，我很清楚，您可能必须单击cookie弹出窗口之外的位置当您也来到TechCrunch时，请随时在评论中告诉我有关这一点的讽刺意味）。毕竟，GitHub拥有付费产品，并且受众可能会使用扩展程序来阻止跟踪器和不必要的cookie。因此，无论如何它收集的跟踪数据可能并没有那么有用。但是，GitHub是最早采取这一举措的大型站点之一，并且也许可以树立趋势。
note_en:
“No one likes cookie banners,” GitHub CEO Nat Friedman writes in today’s announcement. “But cookie banners are everywhere!”
 The reason for that, of course, is because of regulations like GDPR in the U.S. and the EU’s directive to give users the right to refuse the use of cookies that reduce their online privacy. The result, even though these regulations have the users’ best interest in mind, is the constant barrage of cookie banners you experience today.
  “At  GitHub, we want to protect developer privacy, and we find cookie banners irritating, so we decided to look for a solution. After a brief search, we found one: just don’t use any non-essential cookies. Pretty simple, really,” Friedman writes.
 To be fair, for a service like GitHub, it may be a bit easier to do away with cookies than for most sites — and especially content sites (and yes, I’m well aware that you probably had to click away from a cookie popup when you came to TechCrunch, too. Feel free to tell me about the irony of that in the comments). GitHub, after all, has a paid product and an audience that likely uses extensions to block trackers and unnecessary cookies anyway. Because of this, the tracking data it gathered was probably not all that useful anyway. GitHub is one of the first large sites to make this move, though, and may be able to set a bit of a trend.
title: GitHub表示将取消所有不必要的cookie，并且将不再在GitHub.com及其子域上使用“刺激性” cookie横幅
title_en: GitHub says it is doing away with all non-essential cookies and will no longer feature “irritating” cookie banners on GitHub.com and its subdomains
